options: A. Atropine, B. Nicotine, C. Pilocarpine, D. Pyridostigmine

A: #### C.

A: #### A.
Q: Most common complication of celiac plexus block –
options: A. Pneumothorax, B. Postural hypotension, C. Retroperitoneal hemorrhage, D. Intra-arterial injection
A: #### B.
Q: {question}
A:

openlifescienceai/medmcqa::Biochemistry.output_format:
Q: Which is a natural uncoupler?
options: A. Thermogenin, B. 2, 4 dinitrophenol, C. Oligomycin, D. Atractyloside
A: #### A.
Q: Glycogenolysis is best described by which of the following statements:
options: A. It involves enzymes cleaving beta(1- 4) glycosidic linkage, B. Requires activation of glycogen synthase, C. Requires a bifunctional enzyme (debranching and transferase), D. Requires inactivation of phosphorylase kinase
A: #### C.
Q: In a human genome project, scientist notices that one strand of the DNA molecule contains 20 thymine (T), 25 cytosines (C), 30 guanines (G) and 22 adenine (A) residues. How many of each of the bases are found in the complete double-stranded molecule?
options: A. T=44, C=60, G=50, A=40, B. T=22, C=30, G=25, A=20, C. T=40, C=50, G=60, A=44, D. T=42, C=55, G=55, A=42
A: #### C.
Q: Fe absorption increases by which vitamin?
options: A. Vitamin A, B. Vitamin C, C. Thiamine, D. Riboflavin
A: #### B.
Q: Which of the following dietarily non-essential amino acid becomes dietarily essential in patients of homocystinuria?
options: A. Cysteine, B. Methionine, C. Homocysteine, D. Phenylalanine
A: #### A.
Q: {question}
A:

A: #### B.
Q: "Cannon" - a wave is seen in-
options: A. Complete hea block, B. Left ventricular hyperophy, C. Mitral stenosis, D. atrial fibrillation
A: #### A.
Q: {question}
A:
